free_text: Experimental cross sections for light-charged particle

correction procedure by Monte Carlo simulation program
Fast neutron facility of the Louvain-
The 65-MeV incident proton beam is focused
on a 3-mm-thick natural lithium target. With a beam
intensity of 10.E-5 A, about 10E+6 neutrons/s are
available at the location of reaction chamber (about
3.3 m downstream of the lithium target).
The neutron energy spectrum at zero degree consists
of a well-defined peak (2 MeV full-width-at-half-
maximum) containing about 50% of the neutrons, plus
a flat continuum of lower energy neutrons
telescopes were used simultaneously. Each of them
consists of DE detector (NE102 plastic scintillator)
Coincidence between E and DE detectors
Successive measurements of Al and Al2-O3 samples
Time-of-flight (TOF) signal between a capacitive beam
pickoff located upstream from the neutron producing
target and the dE detector is registered. This signal
is subsequently used to select only those events
associated with the incident neutrons in a desired
energy interval. The time resolution in the experiment
protons and deuterons recoiling respectively from a
polypropylene target were used. The H(n,p) scattering
is registered with good statistics, for a precise
determination of differential cross-sections to be
Corrections were made on the sample thickness for
Elemental Al and Al2-O3, each 5*5 cm2 surface,
Total error includes statistical and systema-
The uncertainty of the cross section
absolute scale is about 7-8%, given by:
Error of the measured ref.H(n,p) cross section
Error of the solid angle correction
Error of the number of the sample nuclei
Statistical error in the H(n, p) recoil
proton peak and some others, not specified
The overall relative errors of the points in the
spectra are about 5% for protons and 9% for deuterons
for 62.7MeV data, 14% for protons and 22% for deuterons
for all the other incident neutron energies. They are
given by the statistics in the spectra except for lower
ejectile energies where they can be bigger due to
* EN-RSL - half width of the incident neuron energy bin
